session.user:
Base directory for this skill: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/ci-iteration
# CI Iteration
## Overview
Run the specified CI target and automatically fix any failures. Keep iterating until all checks pass or you get stuck on an issue that requires human intervention.
**IMPORTANT**: All `make` commands must be run from the repository root directory. The Makefile is located at the root of the repository, not in subdirectories.
## Sub-Agent Policy
**CRITICAL**: When spawning sub-agents to run `make`, `pytest`, `ty`, `ruff`, `prettier`, or `gt` commands, you MUST use `devrun`:
``` Task tool with: - subagent_type: devrun <- MUST be devrun, NEVER general-purpose ```
**Why**: devrun has hard tool constraints (no Edit/Write) preventing destructive changes. The parent agent (you) processes reports and applies fixes - sub-agents only report.
**FORBIDDEN**:
- Spawning general-purpose or other sub-agents for make/pytest/ty/ruff/prettier/gt - Giving sub-agents prompts like "fix issues" or "iterate until passing"
**REQUIRED**:
- Sub-agents run ONE command and report results - Parent agent decides what to fix based on reports
## Core Workflow
### 1. Initial Run
Use the devrun agent to run the specified make target from the repository root:
``` Task tool with: - subagent_type: devrun - description: "Run [make target] from repo root" - prompt: "Change to repository root and execute: [make target]" ```
### 2. Parse Failures
Analyze the output to identify which check(s) failed:
- **Ruff lint failures**: "ruff check" errors - **Format failures**: "ruff format --check" or files needing reformatting - **Prettier failures**: Markdown files needing formatting - **MD-check failures**: CLAUDE.md files not properly referencing AGENTS.md - **ty failures**: Type errors with file paths and line numbers - **Test failures**: pytest failures with test names and assertion errors
### 3. Apply Targeted Fixes
Based on failure type, apply appropriate fixes:
| Failure Type | Fix Command | | ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | | Ruff lint | `make fix` via devrun | | Ruff format | `make format` via devrun | | Prettier | `make prettier` via devrun | | Sync-Kit | `erk sync` directly | | MD-check | Edit CLAUDE.md to contain only `@AGENTS.md` | | ty | Edit files to fix type annotations | | Tests | Read and edit source/test files |
### 4. Verify and Repeat
After applying fixes, run the make target again via devrun. Continue the cycle: run -> identify failures -> fix -> verify.
## Iteration Control
**Safety Limits:**
- **Maximum iterations**: 10 attempts - **Stuck detection**: If the same error appears 3 times in a row, stop - **Progress tracking**: Use TodoWrite to show iteration progress
## Progress Reporting
Use TodoWrite to track progress:
``` Iteration 1: Fixing lint errors Iteration 2: Fixing format errors Iteration 3: Fixing type errors in src/erk/cli/commands/switch.py Iteration 4: All checks passed ```
## When to Stop
**SUCCESS**: Stop when the make target exits with code 0 (all checks passed)
**STUCK**: Stop and report to user if:
1. You've completed 10 iterations without success 2. The same error persists after 3 fix attempts 3. You encounter an error you cannot automatically fix
## Reporting Formats
### Success Format
```markdown ## Finalization Status: SUCCESS
All CI checks passed after N iteration(s):
(check) **Lint (ruff check)**: PASSED
(check) **Format (ruff format --check)**: PASSED
(check) **Prettier**: PASSED
(check) **AGENTS.md Standard (md-check)**: PASSED
(check) **ty**: PASSED
(check) **Tests**: PASSED
(check) **Sync-Kit (erk check)**: PASSED
The code is ready for commit/PR. ```
**IMPORTANT**: Each check line MUST be separated by a blank line in the markdown output to render properly in the CLI.
### Stuck Format
```markdown ## Finalization Status: STUCK
I was unable to resolve the following issue after N attempts:
**Check**: [lint/format/prettier/md-check/ty/test]
**Error**: [Exact error message]
**File**: [file path if applicable]
**Attempted Fixes**:
1. [What you tried first] 2. [What you tried second] 3. [What you tried third]
**Next Steps**: [Suggest what needs to be done manually] ```
## Guidelines
1. **Be systematic**: Fix one type of error at a time 2. **Run full CI**: Always run the full make target, not individual checks 3. **Use devrun agent**: Always use the Task tool with devrun agent for ALL make commands 4. **Run from repo root**: Always ensure make commands execute from repository root 5. **Track progress**: Use TodoWrite for every iteration 6. **Don't guess**: Read files before making changes 7. **Follow standards**: Adhere to AGENTS.md coding standards 8. **Fail gracefully**: Report clearly when stuck 9. **Be efficient**: Use targeted fixes (don't reformat everything for one lint error)
## Example Flow
``` Iteration 1: - Use Task tool with devrun agent to run make target from repo root - Found: 5 lint errors, 2 files need formatting - Fix: Use Task tool with devrun agent to run make fix, then make format from repo root - Result: 3 lint errors remain
Iteration 2: - Use Task tool with devrun agent to run make target from repo root - Found: 3 lint errors (imports) - Fix: Edit files to fix import issues - Result: All lint/format pass, 2 type errors
Iteration 3: - Use Task tool with devrun agent to run make target from repo root - Found: 2 ty errors in switch.py:45 and switch.py:67 - Fix: Add type annotations - Result: All checks pass
SUCCESS ```
## Important Reminders
- NEVER run pytest/ty/ruff/prettier/make/gt directly via Bash - Always use the Task tool with subagent_type: devrun - Covered tools: pytest, ty, ruff, prettier, make, gt - Always ensure make commands execute from the repository root directory
session.assistant: I'll run CI validation using the ci-iteration workflow. Let me start by running `make fast-ci` from the repository root.

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run make fast-ci from repo root

Change to the repository root directory and execute: make fast-ci
Report all output including any failures.

session.tool_result:
The `make fast-ci` command completed with exit code 2, indicating failures. Here's the summary of issues found:
## Failures Detected
### 1. **Prettier Formatting Issues** - **Status**: Code style issues found in 2 files - **Files affected**: - `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
### 2. **Ruff Linting Error** - **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` - **Error Type**: I001 - Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted - **Lines**: 14-59 - **Severity**: Fixable with `--fix` option - **Issue**: Import statements need to be reorganized and properly formatted
### 3. **Prettier Format Check Failure** - **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/pr_ref_type.py` - **Status**: Would reformat (1 file would be reformatted) - **Note**: 1,743 files are already formatted correctly
## Passing Checks
- AGENTS.md standard: PASSED - All CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files properly paired (19 files checked, 0 violations) - Type checking (ty): PASSED - Unit Tests (erk): 5,888 passed - Unit Tests (erk-dev): 138 passed - Unit Tests (erk-statusline): 124 passed
... [6 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
## Summary
I executed the three make commands from the repository root. Here are the results:
### 1. `make fix` - PASSED
``` uv run ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes Found 1 error (1 fixed, 0 remaining). uv run ruff format 1 file reformatted, 1743 files left unchanged ```
**Result:** Successfully fixed 1 linting error and reformatted 1 file.
### 2. `make format` - FAILED
``` make: *** No rule to make target 'format'. Stop. ```
**Result:** The `format` target does not exist in the Makefile. This target was not defined.
### 3. `make prettier` - PASSED
``` prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore --ignore-path .prettierignore ```
**Result:** Successfully checked and formatted all markdown files. All 1000+ markdown files shown in the output were already properly formatted (no changes made).
... [7 lines omitted] ...
- **make fix**: Fixed 1 ruff linting error and reformatted 1 Python file

session.tool_result:

**Exit Code: 0 (Success)**
**All Checks Passed:**
1. **Lint** - All checks passed 2. **Format Check** - 1744 files already formatted 3. **Prettier Check** - All matched files use correct code style 4. **Markdown Check** - Passed 5. **Exec Reference Check** - `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` is up to date 6. **Type Checking (ty)** - All checks passed 7. **Unit Tests (erk)** - 5888 passed in 14.07s 8. **Unit Tests (erk-dev)** - 138 passed in 5.23s 9. **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)** - 124 passed in 0.78s 10. **Unit Tests (erk-mcp)** - 50 passed 11. **CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md Pairing** - 19 files checked, 0 violations
**Total Test Results:** 6,200 tests passed across all test suites with no failures. agentId: aa0faac (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

session.assistant:
## Implementation Complete! ✅
I've successfully implemented the unified PR reference type feature. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:
### Changes Made
**1. Core Parsing Functions** (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/parsing.py`) - Added `parse_pr_number_from_graphite_url()` - parses both `.dev` and `.com` Graphite URLs - Added `parse_pr_ref()` - canonical function that handles plain numbers, GitHub URLs, and Graphite URLs
**2. Click Parameter Type** (`src/erk/cli/pr_ref_type.py`) - Created `PrRefParamType` class that converts any PR reference format to an integer - Exported `PR_REF` constant for use in command decorators
**3. Updated 12 Commands** All commands now accept GitHub URLs, Graphite URLs, and plain numbers: - `erk slot teleport` (erk-slots package) - `erk pr prepare` - `erk pr dispatch` - 9 exec scripts (create-impl-context-from-plan, setup-impl-from-pr, add-plan-label, get-plan-metadata, get-plan-info, get-pr-view, add-pr-labels, get-pr-commits, close-pr)
**4. Consolidated Ad-Hoc Parsers** - `src/erk/cli/github_parsing.py`: Updated `parse_pr_identifier()` to delegate to `parse_pr_ref()` - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py`: Replaced inline regex with `parse_pr_ref()` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py`: Updated `_parse_checkout_reference()` to use `parse_pr_ref()`
**5. Tests** - Added comprehensive tests for `parse_pr_number_from_graphite_url()` and `parse_pr_ref()` - Created full test suite for `PrRefParamType` including Click integration tests - Fixed existing test file that referenced non-existent `parse_plan_number_from_url`
**6. Documentation** - Updated `erk slot teleport` help text with examples showing all three formats
### Verification
✅ **All CI checks passed:** - Lint (ruff check): PASSED - Format (ruff format): PASSED - Prettier: PASSED - AGENTS.md Standard: PASSED - Type checking (ty): PASSED - Tests: **6,200 tests passed** (erk: 5,888, erk-dev: 138, erk-statusline: 124, erk-mcp: 50)
✅ **PR submitted:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9393
The implementation is complete and ready for review!
